{"query":"US Commerce Department export controls status Claude Fable 5 Mythos 5 global release date","model":"mako","response_time_ms":1920,"verdict":null,"extra_info":null,"results":[{"url":"https://www.anthropic.com/news/fable-mythos-access","title":"Statement on the US government directive to suspend access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 \ Anthropic","content":"To date, the government has only given us verbal evidence of a potential narrow, non-universal jailbreak, which essentially consists of asking the model to read a specific codebase and fix any software flaws."},{"url":"https://www.cnbc.com/2026/06/30/anthropic-says-trump-admin-has-lifted-export-controls-on-claude-fable-5-and-mythos-5.html","title":"Anthropic says Trump admin has lifted export controls on Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5","content":"Department of Commerce has lifted the export controls on its Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 models.
Commerce Secretary Howard Lutnick gave Anthropic permission to release Mythos 5 to a select group of companies and federal agencies.
The company, which has been at loggerheads with the White House much of this year, rushed to Washington, D.C., to negotiate with the Trump administration after it received the export control directive on June 12."},{"url":"https://fifthrow.com/blog/us-export-control-order-and-global-suspension-of-fable-5-mythos-5-operationalizing-compliance-as-a-live-mandate","title":"US Export-Control Order and Global Suspension of Fable 5 & Mythos 5: Operationalizing Compliance as a Live Mandate | FifthRow – Autonomous AI Apps for Research, Strategy, Consulting","content":"Jun 14, 2026 — Anthropic's suspension of Fable 5 and Mythos 5 began at 5:21 p.m. ET on June 12, 2026, triggered by a legally binding US government export- ...Read more
On June 12, 2026, the global enterprise risk landscape transformed overnight.
ET on June 12, 2026, triggered by a legally binding US government export-control directive.
Platforms spanning AWS Bedrock, Google Cloud, Microsoft Foundry, Snowflake, Box, and the direct Claude APIs were affected simultaneously."},{"url":"https://www.forbes.com/sites/anishasircar/2026/06/16/anthropic-disabled-fable-5-and-mythos-5-after-a-us-export-control-order-heres-what-happened/","title":"Anthropic Disabled Fable 5 And Mythos 5 After A U.S. Export-Control Order. Here’s What Happened","content":"On the evening of 12 June 2026, three days after Anthropic had launched its most capable AI models to the public, the U.S. government ordered them taken down.
“The US government, citing national security authorities, has issued an export control directive to suspend all access to Fable 5 and Mythos 5 by any foreign national, whether inside or outside the United States, including foreign national Anthropic employees,” Anthropic said in its official statement posted shortly after the order arrived Friday evening.
All other Claude models — Opus, Sonnet, Haiku — remain available.
The trigger seemed to be a reported technique for bypassing Fable 5's safety guardrails — the filters used to stop the model from accessing the advanced cybersecurity capabilities baked into the underlying Mythos architecture.
CEO Andy Jassy reportedly told Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent and other government officials that Amazon researchers used Anthropic’s Claude Fable 5 to obtain information that could be used in cyberattacks.
Meanwhile, Sacks pushed back on the idea that old grievances were driving the export control order."},{"url":"https://www.linkedin.com/pulse/tai-209-claude-fable-5-arrived-us-government-ll0xc","title":"TAI #209: Claude Fable 5 Arrived, Then the US Government Took It Offline","content":"The next day, June 12 at 5:21 p.m.
Fable scored 95% on SWE-bench Verified, 80% on the harder SWE-bench Pro, 84.3% on Terminal-Bench 2.1, and 85% on OSWorld-Verified.
Its 29.3% on FrontierCode Diamond was more than double Claude Opus 4.8's 13.4%.
The June 15 emergency talks ended without the controls being lifted, though Commerce officials were reportedly open to restoring access if Anthropic resolved the concern.
Procurement should now ask vendors about export-control exposure, identity requirements, data retention, regional availability, and the provider's right to withdraw a model.
Anthropic launched Claude Fable 5 and Claude Mythos 5 on June 9, then disabled both models for all customers three days later after receiving a US export control directive on June 12.
Over 80 cybersecurity executives and technical leaders signed an open letter on June 14 asking the Commerce Department to lift the restrictions."},{"url":"https://www.digitalapplied.com/blog/fable-5-mythos-us-export-controls-ai-sovereignty-2026","title":"Fable 5 & Mythos Hit US Export Controls: What It Means","content":"Jun 20, 2026 — On June 12, 2026, US export controls forced Anthropic to disable Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 worldwide — three days after the models launched.Read more"},{"url":"https://codersera.com/blog/when-is-claude-fable-5-coming-back-2026/","title":"Is Claude Fable 5 Back? Yes, Restored July 1 2026","content":"Jun 25, 2026 — Anthropic redeployed Claude Fable 5 and Mythos 5 on July 1, 2026, after the U.S. Commerce Department lifted its export-control order on June 30.Read more"}]}

She said her performance was inspired by the condition [spasmodic dysphonia](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spasmodic_dysphonia \"Spasmodic dysphonia\"), a condition that causes a person's voice to go into periods of [spasm](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Spasm \"Spasm\"). Due to the track's popularity upon the trailer's release, it was edited into the final cut of the film, appearing during the climax.[[20]](#cite_note-20) [Waxwork Records](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Waxwork_Records \"Waxwork Records\") announced in August 2019 that they would release the soundtrack to the film on vinyl, which contained Abels's popular score.[[21]](#cite_note-21) The [N.W.A](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/N.W.A \"N.W.A\") song \"[Fuck tha Police](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Fuck_tha_Police \"Fuck tha Police\")\" is also heard when Kitty Tyler tells the voice assistant Ophelia to \"call the police\", moments before she is killed by her Tethered counterpart, Dahlia, and the song plays throughout when the Wilsons enter the Tyler family's home. Peele later explained in the film's digital release special features that a central theme of the film is [American privilege](//en.wikipedia.org/wiki/First_World_privilege \"First World privilege\"):[[66]](#cite_note-SF_Privilege-66).","score":0.0116793895,"raw_content":null}],"response_time":1.13,"request_id":"e39f5e7f-fee7-4d67-8827-0f2e9b2af455"}
